message:
chore(cargo): refresh lockfile for fcp-auth-schema + trybuild additions

Lockfile entries reflect three dependency graph changes from the
recent br-8n0rm / br-jkcka commit sequence:

1. New workspace crate `fcp-auth-schema` (version 0.1.0) introduced
   by 0d7ea88b (feat(fcp-auth-schema): new crate with AuthClaims +
   canonical CBOR) with direct deps chrono, ciborium, serde,
   thiserror.
2. `fcp-auth-schema` is now a direct dep of `fcp-core` and
   `fcp-crypto` after 190b334d (refactor(fcp-crypto/cose): consume
   fcp-auth-schema labels + add with_claims) — lockfile entries for
   both crates now list fcp-auth-schema alongside their existing
   deps.
3. New dev-dep `trybuild` (+ transitive `target-triple`) added to
   fcp-core by 4eeb5d2c (test(fcp-core): trybuild compile-fail tests
   for typestate split). trybuild is the standard Rust compile-fail
   test harness used to pin typestate API misuse at the
   trait-bound level.

Only Cargo.lock was regenerated; no Cargo.toml diffs in this commit
(those landed with the feature commits that introduced the deps).
Keeps CI deterministic and avoids a noisy cargo re-resolve on the
next build.

files.patch:
@@ -2406,6 +2406,16 @@ dependencies = [
  "tracing",
 ]
 
+[[package]]
+name = "fcp-auth-schema"
+version = "0.1.0"

+ "ciborium",
+ "serde",
+ "thiserror 2.0.18",
+]
+

 version = "0.1.0"
